summary: Iteration 258: Backend testing of Zoho Mail email delivery fixes for partner signup, customer signup, and forgot password flows. The three fixes (platform fallback logic, from_email fix, store_name resolution) are verified WORKING — 13 emails were sent successfully via Zoho Mail with correct store_names before the Zoho OAuth token became exhausted. A secondary bug was identified: email_service.py refreshes the Zoho access_token on EVERY email send, causing the token to become invalid after ~13 rapid uses.

critical.issue: Zoho OAuth access_token is NOT cached. Every email send calls refresh_access_token(). After ~13 rapid token refreshes in a session, Zoho returns 400 (token exhausted/rate-limited/rotated). All subsequent emails fail with: 'Zoho token refresh failed: Client error 400 for url https://accounts.zoho.com/oauth/v2/token'. Fix: cache the access_token in oauth_connections.credentials.access_token + access_token_expires_at in DB, and only refresh when expired.

minor.issue: log_entry['provider'] is set to 'zoho_mail' AFTER send_email(), but AFTER token refresh exception, it remains 'mocked'. Should set provider='zoho_mail' at the START of the Zoho branch (before token refresh) to correctly log which provider was attempted.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add Zoho access_token caching in email_service.py — store access_token + expiry in oauth_connections.credentials in DB. On every email send: if cached token exists and not expired, use it directly; else call refresh_access_token() and save the new token+expiry. This prevents rapid token refresh exhaustion.
MINOR: Set log_entry['provider'] = 'zoho_mail' at the START of the Zoho branch in email_service.py (before the token refresh try block) so failed attempts still correctly log provider=zoho_mail.
OPTIONAL: If Zoho returns a new refresh_token in the token refresh response, save it to DB (handles rotating refresh token configurations).
critical_code_review_comments: email_service.py: Zoho access_token is NOT cached. Every email send triggers a fresh token refresh call to Zoho's OAuth endpoint. This causes: (1) unnecessary latency on every email send (~300-500ms per token refresh), (2) Zoho rate limiting/exhaustion after repeated calls, (3) production reliability risk if multiple users register simultaneously. FIX: store access_token + expires_at in oauth_connections.credentials in MongoDB, reuse if valid, only refresh when expired.

email_service.py line 682: log_entry['provider'] = 'zoho_mail' is set AFTER send_email() call. When token refresh fails (exception caught at line 659-663), log_entry['provider'] remains 'mocked' — misleading. Should be set at the START of the elif zoho_mail_conn: block.

auth.py resend_verification_email: The endpoint returns HTTP 200 even when EmailService.send() returns status=failed. Consider returning a non-200 or including email_status in response for transparency.

auth.py forgot_password: Wraps entire try block with bare 'except Exception: pass' which silently swallows all errors including DB errors. While intentional for security (prevent email enumeration), it also hides critical internal errors.

verified_scenarios.platform_fallback_logic: PASS — When tenant has no email provider (test-hardening-fix4 has no zoho_mail/resend connection), platform (automate-accounts) Zoho Mail is used for email delivery
verified_scenarios.store_name_in_subject_automate_accounts: PASS — partner signup (tenant_id=None) uses 'Automate Accounts' store_name: 'Verify your Automate Accounts account'
verified_scenarios.store_name_in_subject_partner_tenant: PASS — customer signup with partner_code uses tenant store_name: 'Verify your TEST_HardeningOrg account'
verified_scenarios.store_name_in_subject_password_reset: PASS — 'Reset your TEST_HardeningOrg password' and 'Reset your Automate Accounts password'
verified_scenarios.no_more_blank_store_name: PASS — Old emails showed 'Verify your  account' (blank). After fix, all subjects have correct store_name
verified_scenarios.from_email_exists: PASS — rushabh@automateaccounts.com is a valid Zoho Mail from_email (old fix: non-existent address caused 500 error)
verified_scenarios.platform_zoho_connection_validated: PASS — automate-accounts has is_validated=True Zoho Mail connection with from_email=rushabh@automateaccounts.com
verified_scenarios.platform_store_name_not_empty: PASS — website_settings for automate-accounts: store_name='Automate Accounts'
verified_scenarios.partner_tenant_no_email_provider: PASS — test-hardening-fix4 has NO email connections, confirming fallback tests are valid
verified_scenarios.zoho_token_refresh_exhaustion: FAIL — After ~13 rapid token refreshes, Zoho returns 400. All subsequent email sends fail. Needs access_token caching in email_service.py.

success_rate.backend: 66% (21/32 tests passed — 11 failures are all due to Zoho token exhaustion from repeated test runs, NOT from the fixes being wrong)

test_credentials: Platform Super Admin: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123! | Partner code: test-hardening-fix4 (TEST_HardeningOrg)
seed_data_creation: Created and auto-cleaned: testiter258_* pending_partner_registrations and unverified users
context_for_next_testing_agent: The three email fixes (fallback, store_name, from_email) ARE WORKING and VERIFIED via DB email_logs. Key DB evidence: 13 emails sent with provider=zoho_mail, status=sent, correct store_names before token was exhausted by repeated test runs. The secondary bug is Zoho access_token not being cached in email_service.py. After main agent adds token caching, re-run this test file. The Zoho oauth_connection for automate-accounts CURRENTLY has an invalid/exhausted refresh_token — main agent needs to re-validate the Zoho Mail connection in the platform admin settings to get a fresh token before retesting email delivery.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Zoho OAuth refresh_token became invalid after repeated rapid use. email_service.py calls refresh_access_token() on EVERY email send, with no caching of the resulting access_token.
reproduction_steps: 2. Observe email_logs: first few show status=sent, provider=zoho_mail
3. After ~13 total sends (across all sessions), observe status=failed, error='Zoho token refresh failed: 400'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: In email_service.py, before calling oauth_svc.refresh_access_token(), check if creds.get('access_token') exists and creds.get('access_token_expires_at') is still in the future. If yes, use cached token. If not, call refresh, then save: await db.oauth_connections.update_one({'tenant_id': platform_tenant, 'provider': 'zoho_mail'}, {'$set': {'credentials.access_token': new_token, 'credentials.access_token_expires_at': expiry_iso}})
rca_of_the_issue.note: The main agent should also re-validate the Zoho Mail connection in platform admin settings (Connected Services) to get a fresh refresh_token for the automate-accounts tenant, since the current one is exhausted.
